entry.title: Warlock Gang Breaches SmarterTools Via SmarterMail Bugs
entry.content:
The ransomware group breached SmarterTools through a vulnerability in the company's own SmarterMail product.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: OT Attacks Get Scary With 'Living-off-the-Plant' Techniques
entry.content:
Ironically, security by obscurity has helped prevent dangerous OT attacks in recent years. It won't be that way forever.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Microsoft Patches 6 Actively Exploited Zero-Days
entry.content:
Three of those zero-days are security feature bypass flaws, which give attackers a way to slip past built-in protections in multiple Microsoft products.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: SolarWinds WHD Attacks Highlight Risks of Exposed Apps
entry.content:
Organizations that have exposed their instances of Web Help Desk to the public Internet have inadvertently made them prime targets for attackers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Top Cyber Industry Defenses Spike CO2 Emissions
entry.content:
Organizations can improve their climate footprints by optimizing two specific cybersecurity protections, without incurring added risks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: AI Rising: Do We Know Enough About the Data Populating It?
entry.content:
Organizations remain reluctant to address the fact that AI can dangerously expose business operations as well as personal data.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: How to Stay on Top of Future Threats With a Cutting-Edge SOC
entry.content:
CISOs should focus on harnessing and securing AI and building new skills among their people. Vision and change management can transform security.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Senegalese Data Breaches Expose Lack of 'Security Maturity'
entry.content:
Green Blood Group steals personal records and biometric data of the West African nation's nearly 20 million residents.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: SpecterOps Launches BloodHound Scentry to Accelerate the Practice of Identity Attack Path Management
entry.content:
Drawing on years of adversary tradecraft, SpecterOps experts work alongside customers to analyze and eliminate attack paths, protect critical assets, and stay ahead of emerging threats.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Booz Allen Announces General Availability of Vellox Reverser to Automate Malware Defense
entry.content:
The AI-powered product delivers expert-grade malware analysis and reverse engineering in minutes.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ivanti EPMM Zero-Day Bugs Spark Exploit Frenzy — Again
entry.content:
It's time to phase out the "patch and pray" approach, eliminate needless public interfaces, and enforce authentication controls, one expert says.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: GitGuardian Raises $50 Million for Secrets and Non-Human Identity Security
entry.content:
The secrets security company has raised more than $100 million since its creation in 2017. The post GitGuardian Raises $50 Million for Secrets and Non-Human Identity Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Webinar Today: Identity Under Attack – Strengthen Your Identity Defenses
entry.content:
Gain practical insights on balancing security, user experience, and operational efficiency while staying ahead of increasingly sophisticated threats. The post Webinar Today: Identity Under Attack – Strengthen Your Identity Defenses appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Nevada Unveils New Statewide Data Classification Policy Months After Cyberattack
entry.content:
Officials said data will now be classified as one of four categories: “public,” “sensitive,” “confidential” or “restricted.” The post Nevada Unveils New Statewide Data Classification Policy Months After Cyberattack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Apple Patches iOS Zero-Day Exploited in ‘Extremely Sophisticated Attack’
entry.content:
Impacting the ‘dyld’ system component, the memory corruption issue can be exploited for arbitrary code execution. The post Apple Patches iOS Zero-Day Exploited in ‘Extremely Sophisticated Attack’ appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hacktivists, State Actors, Cybercriminals Target Global Defense Industry, Google Warns
entry.content:
Threat actors from Russia, China, North Korea and Iran have been observed launching attacks. The post Hacktivists, State Actors, Cybercriminals Target Global Defense Industry, Google Warns appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Microsoft to Enable ‘Windows Baseline Security’ With New Runtime Integrity Safeguards
entry.content:
Windows will have runtime safeguards enabled by default, ensuring that only properly signed software runs. The post Microsoft to Enable ‘Windows Baseline Security’ With New Runtime Integrity Safeguards appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ApolloMD Data Breach Impacts 626,000 Individuals
entry.content:
The company says hackers stole the personal information of patients of affiliated physicians and practices. The post ApolloMD Data Breach Impacts 626,000 Individuals appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: China Revives Tianfu Cup Hacking Contest Under Increased Secrecy
entry.content:
Rewards for exploits are reportedly much smaller than in the contest’s glory days. The post China Revives Tianfu Cup Hacking Contest Under Increased Secrecy appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: US wants cyber partnerships to send ‘coordinated, strategic message’ to adversaries
entry.content:
National Cyber Director Sean Cairncross told attendees of the Munich Cyber Security Conference that Washington is looking to deepen cooperation with partners rather than act alone.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: California fines Disney $2.75 million for data privacy violations
entry.content:
Disney has agreed to pay a $2.75 million to the state of California over allegations that it broke the state’s landmark privacy law by making it exceedingly difficult for consumers to opt out of having their data shared and sold.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: A hard truth in Munich: Cyber defense runs through Silicon Valley
entry.content:
The next arena of political conflict won’t be defined by borders or territory. It will be written in code. And much of that code isn’t controlled by governments at all — it belongs to American companies.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Dutch mobile phone giant Odido announces data breach
entry.content:
In a statement about the incident, Odido CEO Søren Abildgaard said names, bank account numbers, addresses, mobile numbers, email addresses, account numbers and IDs were stolen.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Dismantling Defenses: Trump 2.0 Cyber Year in Review
entry.content:
The Trump administration has pursued a staggering range of policy pivots this past year that threaten to weaken the nation’s ability and willingness to address a broad spectrum of technology challenges, from cybersecurity and privacy to countering disinformation, fraud and corruption. These shifts, along with the president’s efforts to restrict free speech and freedom of the press, have come at such a rapid clip that many readers probably aren’t even aware of them all.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Happy 16th Birthday, KrebsOnSecurity.com!
entry.content:
KrebsOnSecurity.com celebrates its 16th anniversary today! A huge "thank you" to all of our readers -- newcomers, long-timers and drive-by critics alike. Your engagement this past year here has been tremendous and truly a salve on a handful of dark days. Happily, comeuppance was a strong theme running through our coverage in 2025, with a primary focus on entities that enabled complex and globally-dispersed cybercrime services.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Pathway Reads and Data Analysis
entry.content:
Verification alert Listings attributed to AKIRA have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: AKIRA | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

entry.title: Crazy ransomware gang abuses employee monitoring tool in attacks
entry.content:
A member of the Crazy ransomware gang is abusing legitimate employee monitoring software and the SimpleHelp remote support tool to maintain persistence in corporate networks, evade detection, and prepare for ransomware deployment. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Apple fixes zero-day flaw used in 'extremely sophisticated' attacks
entry.content:
Apple has released security updates to fix a zero-day vulnerability that was exploited in an "extremely sophisticated attack" targeting specific individuals. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Google says hackers are abusing Gemini AI for all attacks stages
entry.content:
Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has published a new report warning about AI model extraction/distillation attacks, in which private-sector firms and researchers use legitimate API access to systematically probe models and replicate their logic and reasoning. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Odido data breach exposes personal info of 6.2 million customers
entry.content:
Dutch telecommunications provider Odido is warning that it suffered a cyberattack that reportedly exposed the personal data of 6.2 million customers. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Romania's oil pipeline operator Conpet confirms data stolen in attack
entry.content:
Romania's national oil pipeline operator, Conpet S.A., confirmed that the Qilin ransomware gang stole company data in an attack last week. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Critical BeyondTrust RCE flaw now exploited in attacks, patch now
entry.content:
A critical pre-authentication remote code execution vulnerability in BeyondTrust Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access appliances is now being exploited in attacks after a PoC was published online. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: CodeRED Emergency Notification System Incident Update
entry.content:
It’s easy to forget that just because a news cycle may be about 15 minutes, incident response is much longer. The following is an update from St. Mary’s County in Maryland about the CodeRED emergency systems ransomware attack that affected numerous EMS systems throughout the country. The attack had occurred in CodeRED’s legacy OnSolve environment... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Hacktivist scrapes over 500,000 stalkerware customers’ payment records
entry.content:
Some may experience some schadenfraude over this one. Zack Whittaker reports: A hacktivist has scraped more than half-a-million payment records from a provider of consumer-grade “stalkerware” phone surveillance apps, exposing the email addresses and partial payment information of customers who paid to spy on others. The transactions contain records of payments for phone-tracking services like... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Singapore says China-backed hackers targeted its four largest phone companies
entry.content:
Zack Whittaker reports: Singapore’s government has blamed a known Chinese cyber-espionage group for targeting four of its top telecommunication companies as part of a months-long attack. In a statement Monday, Singapore confirmed for the first time that the hackers, known as UNC3886, targeted the country’s telecoms infrastructure, including its largest companies: Singtel, StarHub, M1, and Simba... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: South Korea government is promoting bill for statutory damages for personal information leaks.
entry.content:
Kwon Soon-wan reports: The Democratic Party of Korea and the government are pushing for a bill that would hold companies liable for damages in personal information leaks even in the absence of intent or negligence. It was reported on the 10th that the ruling and government parties intend to apply this law retroactively to past... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Maine House Passes Strong Privacy Bill
entry.content:
EPIC writes: A strong comprehensive privacy bill passed the Maine House of Representatives today. The bill, LD 1822, closely mirrors the privacy law Maryland passed in 2024 and would extend essential privacy protections to Mainers. The bill includes strong data minimization requirements, enhanced protections for sensitive data, and civil rights protections prohibiting data-driven discrimination. EPIC has testified in support of... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Cybersecurity Information Sharing Act of 2015 Reauthorized Through September 2026
entry.content:
Ashden Fein, Jess Gonzalez Valenzuela, Analese Bridges, John Webster Leslie, and Claire O’Rourke of Covington and Burling write: The Cybersecurity Information Sharing Act of 2015 (“CISA 2015”), which provides liability protections and other safeguards for sharing certain cybersecurity information with the U.S. federal government and private entities, was reauthorized as part of the funding bill... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Comcast agrees to $117.5M data breach class action settlement
entry.content:
Top Class Actions reports a settlement stemming from the Citrix Bleed” data breach it suffered in 2023. NOTE: You cannot sign up for a claim at this time. Nor can you sign up via DataBreaches.net. Wait for the official settlement site and follow the instructions on that site to find out if you are eligible... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Kettering Adventist Health now notifying patients affected by May 2025 ransomware attack
entry.content:
On May 20, 2025, CNN reported that a ransomware attack had triggered a “system-wide technology outage” at Kettering Adventist Health in Ohio. Disclosures by Kettering Health would later explain that the healthcare system had been attacked by InterLock, a ransomware gang who were threatening to destroy data and publish data if their demands were not... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: ApolloMD reveals that 626,540 patients were affected by May, 2025 cyberattack
entry.content:
There is an update to a breach previously reported on DataBreaches.net. ApolloMD describes itself as a private, independent group of physicians that partners with more than 100 hospitals nationwide to provide integrated, multispecialty physician, Ambulatory Payment Classifications (APCs), and practice management services. As such, they are business associates of HIPAA-covered entities. This week, the Georgia-based... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: It’s 2026, but hospitals still haven’t prevented snooping in celebrities’ records
entry.content:
DataBreaches is not on TikTok and, being something of a dinosaur, never heard of “Josh and Jase” before. But no patient should have their privacy violated the ways Josh’s was. What happened to “break the glass?” What happened to all the software and auditing protections to prevent hospital employees from snooping on celebrity patients’ records?... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: McLaren Health Care Pays $14 Million to Settle Litigation Over Ransomware Attacks
entry.content:
McLaren Health Care has agreed to pay $14 million to settle class action litigation stemming from two ransomware attacks in […] The post McLaren Health Care Pays $14 Million to Settle Litigation Over Ransomware Attacks appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Precipio; Pit River Health Service; Tulane University Medical Group Confirm Data Breaches
entry.content:
Data breaches have been announced by the Connecticut diagnostic laboratory Precipio, Pit River Health Service in California, and Tulane University […] The post Precipio; Pit River Health Service; Tulane University Medical Group Confirm Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Staten Island University Hospital Settles Lawsuit Over Business Associate Data Breach
entry.content:
Staten Island University Hospital (SIUH) in New York has agreed to settle a class action lawsuit over a 2024 data […] The post Staten Island University Hospital Settles Lawsuit Over Business Associate Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: 83,000 Clients Affected by Cyberattack on Ohio Counseling Center
entry.content:
The Counseling Center of Wayne and Holmes Counties has experienced a cyberattack affecting 83,350 individuals. Data breaches have also been […] The post 83,000 Clients Affected by Cyberattack on Ohio Counseling Center appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Three Healthcare Providers Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits
entry.content:
Settlements have received preliminary approval from the courts to resolve class action lawsuits against Northeast Rehabilitation Hospital Network, American Addiction […] The post Three Healthcare Providers Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: ApolloMD Confirms 626,500 Patients Affected by May 2025 Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
The extent of a May 2025 ransomware attack on the Georgia-based physician- and clinician-owned staffing and management group ApolloMD has […] The post ApolloMD Confirms 626,500 Patients Affected by May 2025 Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Pinehurst Radiology Associates & Tallahassee Memorial HealthCare Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits
entry.content:
Pinehurst Radiology Associates has agreed to settle a class action lawsuit over a January 2025 data breach, and Tallahassee Memorial […] The post Pinehurst Radiology Associates & Tallahassee Memorial HealthCare Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Data Breaches Announced by MedRevenu & EyeCare Partners
entry.content:
Data breaches have been confirmed by the revenue cycle management company MedRevenu Inland Physicians Hospitalist Services, and the Missouri-based eye […] The post Data Breaches Announced by MedRevenu & EyeCare Partners appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Healthcare Remains the Sector Most Targeted by Ransomware Groups as Attacks Increase 49% YOY
entry.content:
A new record was set for ransomware attacks last year, with disclosed ransomware attacks increasing by 49% year-over-year to a […] The post Healthcare Remains the Sector Most Targeted by Ransomware Groups as Attacks Increase 49% YOY appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach
